title: The Bracelet

jokes.text: What a moron! lt's only two yards that you're gonna get. So what? You don't need two yards.
jokes.setup: Football player makes a strategic error on TV
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly agitated commentary about obvious game management
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsessing over minutiae - his passionate anger at sports incompetence establishes his character immediately

jokes.text: The prostitutes don't mind it. / They give you a discount? / They do, they get off on it.
jokes.setup: Cheryl comments on Larry's scruff being cute
jokes.punchline: Larry casually escalates to prostitutes enjoying his facial hair and giving discounts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts innocent, Larry's casual delivery of increasingly absurd details about prostitute preferences is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Somebody told me today that too much soy makes your brain shrink. Kind of scared me a little bit. l've been having soy for breakfast with my cereal, everyday for the last 10 years.
jokes.setup: Larry shares random health concern about soy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been consuming it daily for a decade, implying his brain may already be compromised
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's neurotic health anxieties and self-defeating logic

jokes.text: What am l gonna do, switch to milk now? l don't know if l could switch to milk. You know, cholesterol. Can't win, really.
jokes.setup: Larry's dilemma about soy potentially shrinking his brain
jokes.punchline: Gets trapped in circular logic about equally bad milk alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - paralyzed by contradictory health advice, delivered with resigned futility

jokes.text: How's your cousin, Griffin? / Griffin is my nephew.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to show interest in Cheryl's family
jokes.punchline: Gets basic family relationship wrong despite just hearing about it
jokes.explanation: Efficient character beat showing Larry's self-absorbed inattentiveness even when trying to be considerate

jokes.text: l'm sensing rancor. Or is it 'ranker'? 'Rancor'?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to identify Cheryl's mood
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by pronunciation uncertainty of the very word he's using
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry - derailing a serious conversation with pedantic word concerns

jokes.text: How about: 'Honey, l forgive you. Would you like to have sex?' How about that? Or any combination of that. Either forgiveness or the sex. Of course, both would be great. That would be ideal. l'm not expecting both.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks what Larry wants her to say
jokes.punchline: Larry provides increasingly desperate and specific sexual negotiations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's transactional view of relationships and complete social tone-deafness

jokes.text: lf you were watching the Oscars or something, okay... and they were about to announce the best-actress category... and l came home... l would say, 'No, sit down, finish watching it.'
jokes.setup: Larry trying to justify his football watching behavior
jokes.punchline: Uses hypothetical that reveals his complete lack of interest in what Cheryl would care about
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's inability to understand what matters to others - his 'generous' example reveals total disconnect

jokes.text: lf it was in the third quarter... easily, l'd give you 15 minutes. But we're in the last two minutes of the game. They've got the ball on the 35-yard line.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued justification of ignoring Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly specific about football logistics while missing the relationship point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - treats relationship crisis like sports commentary, completely missing emotional context

jokes.text: You haven't shaved? / All right, what's going on? / Nothing. / No, something's up with you.
jokes.setup: Richard notices Larry's disheveled appearance
jokes.punchline: Richard immediately recognizes this as a sign of relationship trouble
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Richard as the neurotic friend who immediately spots relationship distress signals

jokes.text: You gotta get her a gift. You can't come home empty-handed.
jokes.setup: Larry explains the football situation
jokes.punchline: Richard immediately goes to materialistic solution
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main plot while showing Richard's transactional view of relationships

jokes.text: Yeah, that's it. That's what l was afraid of.
jokes.setup: Richard describes expensive platinum bracelet with diamonds
jokes.punchline: Larry's resigned acceptance of his expensive fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry resignation - already dreading the financial implications before he's even committed

jokes.text: You're that chicken l'orange guy. Oh, yeah! How you doing? Good, how are you? Good to see you. Nice to see you. Thanks a lot for that chicken, that was highly appreciated.
jokes.setup: Homeless man recognizes Larry from previous encounter
jokes.punchline: Awkward enthusiastic greeting about food Larry apparently gave him
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing Larry's ongoing relationship with homeless community, creates awkward social obligation

jokes.text: You wouldn't have any spare change? / No. / Here's a piece of gum.
jokes.setup: Homeless man asks for money
jokes.punchline: Offers gum instead, completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry social awkwardness - thinks gum is equivalent exchange for money, completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: No. / l wasn't with that guy. l'm not with him. These are my workout clothes!
jokes.setup: Store employee sees Richard with Larry and homeless man
jokes.punchline: Richard desperately distances himself from the situation
jokes.explanation: Richard's class consciousness and embarrassment at being associated with Larry's social awkwardness

jokes.text: Excuse me, sir, could you do me a favor? l'm gonna dial a number. lf a woman answers the phone, just hang up... and if a man answers, hand it back to me, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry approaches random stranger with phone request
jokes.punchline: Asks stranger to execute elaborate scheme to avoid talking to Richard's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Larry's elaborate schemes to avoid minor social discomfort, involving innocent strangers

jokes.text: What is this about? / l hate my friend's girlfriend. l don't wanna talk to her.
jokes.setup: Stranger asks for explanation of phone scheme
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest and petty explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry honesty - no social filter, treats stranger like confessor for petty personal problems

jokes.text: You look like the Jewish Ratso Rizzo.
jokes.setup: Richard sees Larry's disheveled appearance
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural reference comparing Larry to iconic street character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description - culturally specific, economical, and perfectly captures Larry's disheveled state

jokes.text: That's the first thank you in over two years. / You haven't done anything to thank you for. / Good point.
jokes.setup: Richard notes Larry's rare expression of gratitude
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately agrees he's generally unthankful
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Larry's complete lack of self-awareness about his ingratitude, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: ls there a name like 'Pluto's Tail'? / l don't know the name of it. / ls it on one of the velvet neck things? / Yeah, it's right in the front center-- / No, it's a necklace-- / lt's a bracelet. lt's not on a hand.
jokes.setup: Richard trying to identify the bracelet
jokes.punchline: Increasingly confused jewelry terminology from both characters
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' incompetence with jewelry shopping, creates realistic confusion

jokes.text: Why'd l ever call this guy?
jokes.setup: Richard continues jewelry confusion
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate regret about involving Richard
jokes.explanation: Larry's instant buyer's remorse about asking Richard for help, delivered as internal monologue

jokes.text: Smarty pants. Closed.
jokes.setup: They arrive at the jewelry store
jokes.punchline: Store is closed, Larry sarcastically blames himself
jokes.explanation: Larry's self-directed sarcasm for poor planning, economical expression of frustration

jokes.text: l told you, 25 relatives had heart attacks in my family. Do me one, let me walk. / Okay, please. / l'm not lying.
jokes.setup: Richard wants to walk to restaurant for exercise
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic health justifications including family death toll
jokes.explanation: Richard's neurotic health anxiety escalating to morbid family statistics to justify walking

jokes.text: Why do you think? We're men, right, and men need it more. No, it's not about that. l got it. Because they are so offended... by our genitalia... that they have to be in love with us to have it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about gender differences in sexual behavior
jokes.punchline: Richard's theory that penises are so grotesque women need emotional attachment to tolerate them
jokes.explanation: Richard's pseudo-intellectual sex theory with self-deprecating male anatomy humor

jokes.text: with their grotesque penis. / l'm missing something. / No, l think it's a good theory. / lt's a disgusting theory at lunch, l'll tell you that.
jokes.setup: Richard continues penis grotesqueness theory
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical complaint about inappropriate lunch conversation
jokes.explanation: Larry's practical objection to Richard's inappropriate timing rather than content

jokes.text: There's a space here to tip a captain? A captain? lnstead of a waiter? No, both. Look at this. There's a space for a captain, and a space for a waiter.
jokes.setup: Larry examining restaurant bill
jokes.punchline: Discovery of multiple tip lines including mysterious 'captain' position
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social observation about restaurant industry tip creep, building outrage

jokes.text: Allotment? / l have 20 percent allotted to the waiter. / Sound like you're in the schmata business. / The captain's gonna be horning in now on my allotment?
jokes.setup: Larry uses business terminology for tipping
jokes.punchline: Richard mocks Larry's formal language, Larry continues with 'horning in' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry treating tipping like corporate budget allocation, with territorial language

jokes.text: What does a captain do? l think l saw. The guy at the podium. He pointed.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to understand captain's role
jokes.punchline: Reduces captain's job to minimal pointing gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reductive observation - expensive service reduced to basic pointing action

jokes.text: Let the waiter give the captain 10 percent if he wants. l'll give the waiter the money.
jokes.setup: Larry's resistance to tipping captain separately
jokes.punchline: Larry's solution to make waiter handle captain payment himself
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical but socially awkward solution to tip structure problem

jokes.text: Next time, there'll be a space for a lieutenant... then maybe a colonel, tip the colonel. 'How much, Colonel?' What does the colonel get?
jokes.setup: Larry's complaint about restaurant tip hierarchy
jokes.punchline: Extends restaurant staff to military ranks with escalating absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation taking restaurant hierarchy to absurd military extreme, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: l bet in school, you didn't put the little penny in the UNlCEF slots? / l put a lot of pennies.
jokes.setup: Richard suggests Larry was selfish as a child
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive response about childhood charity
jokes.explanation: Richard psychoanalyzing Larry's current cheapness through childhood behavior

jokes.text: l can't believe you stopped. l've been standing here for over an hour... and you're the first people that have actually stopped.
jokes.setup: Blind man expresses gratitude for help
jokes.punchline: Reveals this will be much more involved than expected
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating commitment trap - what seems like simple kindness becomes elaborate burden

jokes.text: How can you not help a blind man? / How could you say 'blind man,' in front of a blind man?
jokes.setup: Richard argues they must help the blind man
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror at Richard using the word 'blind' in front of him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry political correctness panic - more concerned with language propriety than actual help

jokes.text: l meant 'sightless.' l respect the blind as much as anybody. / lt's not a problem, believe me. / l didn't mean that in a derogatory sense.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to correct his language
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by using 'blind' again while claiming respect
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overcompensation making the situation more awkward while trying to be sensitive

jokes.text: l got my own problems. l'm a recovering alcoholic. l have intimacy problems. / Poor guy, terrible intimacy problems. / Can't get close to a woman. lt's a terrible thing.
jokes.setup: Larry listing his personal problems
jokes.punchline: Richard joining in to elaborate on Larry's intimacy issues to the blind stranger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of inappropriate oversharing with stranger, Richard making it worse

jokes.text: lt happens to be true. l am just saying, we're all the same. He can't see, and you have intimacy problems. / You guys have a lot in common.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to create equivalence between problems
jokes.punchline: Richard suggesting blindness and intimacy issues are comparable disabilities
jokes.explanation: Absurd false equivalence between physical disability and Larry's psychological issues

jokes.text: Try to think about my fingers once, will you? / l had a colonoscopy two weeks ago. / You have no strength. You're so fucking dainty.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about Larry's moving technique
jokes.punchline: Michael's increasingly harsh criticism including unprovoked medical information and insults
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation - the 'helpless' blind man is actually demanding and abusive

jokes.text: Back up! / You're like a goddamn lion tamer.
jokes.setup: Michael giving increasingly aggressive directions
jokes.punchline: Larry's comparison to circus animal training
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual metaphor for Michael's commanding behavior, economical and specific

jokes.text: Back up! / You're like a goddamn lion tamer.
jokes.setup: Blind man directing Larry's movements
jokes.punchline: Larry compares blind directions to lion taming
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor for the absurd dynamic of blind person directing sighted person

jokes.text: Be well, take care of yourself. / Okay, just the mattress. / Where do you want the mattress? / ln the bedroom.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to leave after helping
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals there's more work, including a mattress
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - just when escape seems possible, more work appears

jokes.text: Just bring the TV in and stick it... in the fireplace there... and go. / Get the TV from the bedroom? / Stick it in the recess of the fireplace.
jokes.setup: Michael wants TV moved again
jokes.punchline: Reveals they just moved it TO the bedroom only to move it back out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the blind man's poor planning and increasing demands

jokes.text: What in the name of God am l gonna do with a TV in my room? l'm blind. / But it doesn't work in here! lt just doesn't fit! / That's where it belongs! Are you questioning my sense of space?
jokes.setup: Larry questioning TV placement logic
jokes.punchline: Blind man defending his spatial design sense while admitting he can't see TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment - blind man defending his interior design choices while acknowledging he can't use the TV

jokes.text: The blind man misled us, though. / lt's true, but-- / He did mislead us. He didn't tell us the truth about what we had to move. He was a deceptive blind man.
jokes.setup: Larry and Richard reflecting on the experience
jokes.punchline: Larry's formal accusation of 'deceptive blind man' behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - treating the blind man like a con artist, formal language for absurd complaint

jokes.text: l think if you're blind, you have some.... You got a license, you got a blind license? / l can finish my own thought-- / You weren't doing a very good job of it.
jokes.setup: Larry struggling to articulate his point about blind privileges
jokes.punchline: Richard's cutting interruption about Larry's poor communication
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Richard's impatience with Larry's rambling thoughts

jokes.text: You helped a blind man, and, to me, this was a great day. / lt was? / Yeah. / Every day's a great day for me. / Look at you, Norman Vincent Lewis.
jokes.setup: Richard finding meaning in helping the blind man
jokes.punchline: Larry's mocking reference combining Norman Vincent Peale with Richard Lewis
jokes.explanation: Clever name-mashing combining positive thinking guru with Richard's neurotic persona

jokes.text: You are never filled with any gratitude. You have no gratitude. / You have more serenity? / ln my own way l do. / l am in pretty bad shape, if that's the case.
jokes.setup: Richard claiming to have more serenity than Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating acknowledgment that this would be concerning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-awareness about his neurotic state

jokes.text: You are just a babbling brook of bullshit.
jokes.setup: Richard's continued self-help philosophizing
jokes.punchline: Larry's creative metaphor for Richard's nonsensical advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor combining natural imagery with profanity - economical and poetic insult

jokes.text: You are just a babbling brook of bullshit.
jokes.setup: Richard's continued preaching about gratitude
jokes.punchline: Larry's poetic insult combining nature metaphor with profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult combining beautiful nature imagery with harsh criticism - perfectly quotable

jokes.text: You didn't happen to pick up my credit card at lunch today, did you? I don't know, I must have left it on the table or something.
jokes.setup: Larry calling restaurant about lost card
jokes.punchline: Sets up return to scene of tipping controversy
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for callback - creates reason to return to restaurant for confrontation

jokes.text: You didn't happen to pick up my credit card at lunch today, did you? / I don't know, I must have left it on the table or something.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes card is missing
jokes.punchline: Discovers another problem while dealing with bracelet issue
jokes.explanation: Setup for next day's restaurant conflict, adds to Larry's mounting problems

jokes.text: Got her some roses. Boy, it's amazing how these roses work.
jokes.setup: Larry showing off his successful reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Treating flowers like a mechanical solution to relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Larry's transactional view of romance - treating gifts like technology that 'works'

jokes.text: And you know what? l passed by that jewelry store on 23rd... and l think, l really do want that bracelet.
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentioning she saw the jewelry store
jokes.punchline: Sets up Larry's upcoming dilemma about the bracelet
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central conflict - dramatic irony as audience knows Larry's dilemma

jokes.text: he was with me today... and he just asked me if he could buy it for his girlfriend... and l guess l kinda told him 'okay.'
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain why he can't buy the bracelet
jokes.punchline: Reveals he gave away Cheryl's desired gift to Richard's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-sabotage - creates his own problem through poor timing and decisions

jokes.text: So, l don't want the bracelet because we made up? / That doesn't make sense. / l know it doesn't, it was stupid.
jokes.setup: Cheryl questioning Larry's logic about the gift
jokes.punchline: Larry admitting his reasoning makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Larry's rare moment of self-awareness about his illogical behavior

jokes.text: No. He's got caller lD, and l'm gonna be on the phone... for five hours, explaining to him why l hung up, and l don't want that.
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests calling Richard to fix the situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate prediction of how calling would create more problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier phone avoidance, shows Larry's paranoia about simple social interactions

jokes.text: l'll get up and go down there. l will be there when they open the store. l will turn the key. l'll greet them in the morning.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to get the bracelet first thing
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic promises culminating in greeting store employees
jokes.explanation: Larry's characteristic over-promising with increasingly specific and dramatic commitments

jokes.text: Normally, when we have good service, we get tips. l think you left something like 30 percent for the waiter... and you just drew a line through my waiter-captain tip.
jokes.setup: Perry confronting Larry about the tip situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals the tipping conflict has real-world consequences
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing real consequences of Larry's tip philosophy confrontation

jokes.text: That waiter didn't share that tip with you? / No, he didn't. / That is so unfair. That's terrible.
jokes.setup: Larry learning about tip-sharing practices
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake outrage about the system he was just complaining about
jokes.explanation: Larry's insincere sympathy while trying to avoid responsibility for his tipping choice

jokes.text: You better find yourself another mode of transportation.
jokes.setup: Larry offers to move Perry's car
jokes.punchline: Perry refuses and traps Larry without transportation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Larry's cheapness literally strands him, delivered with understated threat

jokes.text: Richard! Wait!
jokes.setup: Larry stranded outside the jewelry store
jokes.punchline: Desperately calling for Richard as final hope
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry desperation - all his schemes have backfired leaving him helpless

tmdb_overview: Larry is continually thwarted in his effort to buy a bracelet as a peacemaking gift for Cheryl.
